findings.description: Repeated header/footer content inserted between page-equivalent sections disrupts reading order. Footer text, a colored separator bar, and header names (Pat McCrory/Governor, Donald R. Van Der Vaart/Secretary, Tracy Davis/Director) are inserted between the letterhead image and the MEMORANDUM body content, splitting what was a single PDF page.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows the letterhead and MEMORANDUM content on one continuous page. In the HTML, after the letterhead image there is a large blank space, then the full footer ('State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality…'), a colored bar, then the header names repeated as text, before the MEMORANDUM heading appears. A sighted reader would encounter the footer and duplicated names mid-page.

findings.description: Footer text and colored separator bar inserted between the end of the memo outline (Section I-IV) and the beginning of Section I content, disrupting continuous reading flow.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 ends with the outline and PDF page 2 begins with Section I. In the HTML, between the outline items and the Section I heading, a full footer block and colored bar are inserted, creating a reading interruption not present in the PDF.

findings.description: The header image (612pt wide, described as 'NC DEQ logo and letterhead') likely captures the entire letterhead area including the officials' names and titles. The same names and titles (PAT McCRORY/Governor, DONALD R. VAN DER VAART/Secretary, TRACY DAVIS/Director) are also rendered as separate text paragraphs within the <header> element, resulting in the same content appearing both as an embedded image and as HTML text.
findings.evidence:
HTML header contains: followed by

PAT McCRORY
Governor

,

DONALD R. VAN DER VAART
Secretary

,

TRACY DAVIS
Director

. In the PDF, all of this is part of the single letterhead block on page 1.

findings.description: A second full-page-width image (612pt) with empty alt text is inserted between the Table of Contents and Section I (History/Background), mid-content in the HTML body. This appears to be a page-break artifact or a full-page image that potentially duplicates surrounding content and disrupts reading flow.
findings.evidence:
HTML contains placed between the
TOC and the

heading. No corresponding standalone image appears at this location in the PDF.
